title: Handling Events in React
description: Respond to user actions with event handlers.

slides.title: Events in React ⚡
slides.content:
# Synthetic Events

React normalizes events so they work consistently across browsers. Event handlers are passed a **synthetic event** object, which has the same interface as native browser events.

## Common Events

* `onClick` – when an element is clicked
* `onChange` – when an input value changes
* `onSubmit` – when a form is submitted
* `onMouseEnter` / `onMouseLeave`

Event handlers are passed as props, using camelCase, and assigned a function.

```jsx
<button onClick={() => alert('Clicked!')}>Click</button>
```

slides.title: Event Handler Examples
slides.content: Different ways to define event handlers:

quizData.question: What is the correct way to attach a click handler to a button in React?
quizData.explanation: React uses camelCase event names, so `onClick` (with capital C) is correct.
